ground_truth.title: 10 Extremely Helpful Free Graphic Design Courses
ground_truth.author: Format Team
ground_truth.main_content:
Graphic design is an increasingly important part of every kind of creative's toolkit. If you're working in the visual industry, having an understanding of the Adobe Suite, especially Photoshop and the foundations of design is important for giving your work that extra edge–especially in a competitive market where AI is readily available. For illustrators and artists, graphic design is a fundamental part of the job and careers—all the more reason to keep expanding your design skills and knowledge.

In the age of the internet, improving your skills doesn't have to mean going back to school. There are a number of online graphic design courses offered by universities, graphic design experts, and online course hubs like Udemy, that can help you hone your skills. We've gathered the best free graphic design courses out there to help budding designers and other interested creatives upskill on the foundations of typography, color, and essential graphic design software.

1. CalArts Graphic Design Tutorial For Beginners - An 11 hour beginner course for graphic design presented by the California Institute of Design, this is an incredible resource offered for free on YouTube.

2. Graphic Design CRASH-COURSE (Master The Psychology of Shapes) - Approaching from and emphasizing the psychology of design, this course from Satori Designs establishes the emotional qualities of shapes, and how they can influence the call to action of your design.

3. Alison Online - Alison's stated goal is to make it possible for anyone to study any subject, marketing itself as a new world of free certified learning. Learners can start from anywhere, at any subject level, for free.

4. Udemy - Offering courses taught by independent instructors, Udemy is a competitive classroom marketplace. Classes are priced according to the instructor's experience and skill, and rated by students.

5. The Principles of Design - This free course brilliantly illustrates the fundamentals of design. Moving through balance, pattern, rhythm and into more complex topics like harmony and proportion.

6. Veerle's Graphic Design Blog - Veerle Pieters is a Belgium-based graphic/web designer with a website dedicated to all things graphic design. She has experience designing everything from marketing and branding to logos, stationery, and brochures.

7. Envato Tuts+ Illustration and Design Courses - Envato has created a dense and helpful tutorial archive to help with almost any software and process in design.

8. Creative Pro - Creative Pro is an online design magazine with a helpful How-To section for learning graphic design.

9. Fundamentals of Creative Design from Coursera - This introduction to design is offered through the California Institute of Arts on Coursera. The Fundamentals of Creative Design is a great starting point as it covers topics from typography and image-making, to shape, color, and composition.

10. Demystifying Graphic Design: How Posters Work From Skillshare - Skillshare is an online learning platform that offers courses on a wide range of topics. Ellen Lupton's course explores how posters work and explores moves that make modern graphic design so compelling.

Bonus: A Brief History of Typography - Typography is key to the work of a graphic designer. This well-designed free pdf serves as evidence of its own wisdom, containing a comprehensive introduction to the history and nuances of typography.

In the age of the internet, improving your skills doesn't have to mean going back to school.
